# **Overtime Analysis Revamp PRD**

# **Introduction**

This product requirements document highlights the key requirements of the over-time analysis of different surveys.

At present, the product allows the Super-Admins to view the overtime analysis report in the UI.  
![][image1]

Engagement over time shows how the engagement score has progressed over the selected time period (Monthly/ Quarterly/ Yearly or By Survey)  
![][image2]  
Key Focus Areas show the most and least progressed demographics based on engagement score over the past 2 months.  
![][image3]

eNPS overtime shows how the eNPS has varied in the selected timeframe![][image4]  
The driver score over time shows the progression of the selected driver's score within the selected time frame.![][image5]

Focus points give a dimension-wise outperforming and underperforming entities for each driver ![][image6]

The download report option downloads an xls file with the scores for each time frame selected.  
![][image7]  
![][image8]

# **Customer Concerns**

* Customers want **Sub-admins** (e.g., regional or department heads) to have access to Overtime Analysis, restricted by their assigned hierarchy, so insights are not limited to global admins.  
* The ability to compare overtime data using **both custom and default attributes** is required, enabling analysis that aligns with internal org structures and people dimensions.  
* Customers need **year-over-year comparisons of key drivers**, either across previous surveys or a selected set of surveys, to identify consistent patterns, seasonality, and long-term shifts.  
* A requirement to **export tabular data** showing driver comparisons across custom and default attributes, supporting deeper offline analysis and leadership reporting.  
* Customers are asking for an **auto-generated summary** that highlights significant score changes, major improvements or declines, and notable differences across surveys and years—moving from raw data to decision-ready insights.

# 

# **Product Requirements**

UI Mock : [Overtime\_Revamp\_Mock\_V2.html](https://drive.google.com/file/d/19Sjvz2a2RBArDpM8z7Hsyd-jNR5_3rtM/view?usp=drive_link)

## **FR1: Sub-Admin Access to Overtime Analysis**

As a **Sub-Admin**, I should be able to **access the Overtime Analysis feature** to analyze trends over time for the **surveys and employees within my assigned scope (**location/business group or unit **)**.

* Access should be governed by **role-based and hierarchy-based restrictions** (e.g., teams, departments, regions).  
* Sub-Admins should only view data that meets **configured anonymity thresholds**.  
* Capabilities should mirror Admin access **Sub-Admin permitted scope**, ensuring meaningful analysis without exposing organization-wide data.

## **FR2: Multi-Survey Overtime Analysis Configuration**

### **Entry – Reports Page (Existing)**

Users access the Reports page where completed surveys are listed.

#### **Interaction**

* Each survey row includes a selection checkbox.  
* Users can select multiple surveys.  
* "Analyse Overtime" button activates after selecting at least two surveys.

### **Analysis Configuration Modal**

#### **Step 1: Survey Comparison Setup**

Users configure anchor and comparison surveys.

**Compare (Anchor Survey)**

* Single select dropdown.  
* Default selection is the latest survey based on end date.

**Compare With (Historical Surveys)**

* Multi-select dropdown.  
* Default includes remaining selected surveys.

**Validation Rules**

* Anchor survey cannot be part of comparison set.  
* Minimum one historical survey required.

![][image9]

#### **Attribute & Anonymity Configuration**

**Attribute Selection**

Checkbox selection

* Default attributes  
* Custom attributes

**Anonymity Threshold Configuration**

* Minimum response/participant count.  
* Applies across segmentation and exports.

Users are redirected to a dedicated full-page analytics workspace.  
![][image10]

## **FR3: Multi-Survey Analysis Comparison**

### **Header Section**

Includes:

* Page Title: Overtime Analysis  
* Configure Analysis button  
* Download Report button

### **Comparison Context Banner**

Displays:

* Anchor Survey  
* Comparison Surveys

### **Summary Comparison Section**

Displays a comparison between the anchor survey and aggregated historical surveys.

Metrics included:

* Engagement Score  
* Participation Rate  
* eNPS Score   
  * **Anchor survey has no eNPS** `→ hide eNPS sections entirely`  
  * **Anchor has eNPS \+ some compare surveys don’t** `→ eNPS visible, exclude those surveys from eNPS calcs, show inline note`  
  * **None have eNPS** `→ hide eNPS entirely + disable eNPS metric in export modal`  
* Promoter / Neutral / Detractor Distribution (No card if the surveys don’t have eNPS)

![][image11]

### **Tab Navigation (Existing)**

Tabs:

* Overtime  
* Focus Points

![][image12]

### **Overtime Tab**

A toggle control allows users to switch analytical perspectives.

**Toggle Options**

* Survey Comparison (Default)  
* Time Trend Analysis

![][image13]

### **Survey Comparison Analysis**

#### **Engagement Survey Comparison** 

**Visualization** \- A Slope chart of the selected surveys, arranged from oldest to the left   
**X-axis** \- Surveys   
**Y-axis** \- Score

Tooltip shows:

* Survey Name  
* Score  
* Response Count  
* Survey Month and Year

![][image14]

**Section Download (Image)**

* Engagement section includes: **Download as Image**  
* PNG includes: chart, legend (if present), compare context line, timestamp.

#### **​​eNPS Survey Comparison**

**Axes**

* X Axis: Survey Runs  
* Y Axis: eNPS (-100 to \+100)

**Tooltip Displays**

* eNPS Score  
* Promoter/Neutral/Detractor counts  
* Response count

![][image15]

**Section Download (Image)**

* eNPS section includes: **Download as Image**  
* Export includes the breakdown context (Promoters/Neutrals/Detractors) either within tooltip snapshot area or as a small legend line under chart.

#### **Driver Survey Comparison**

**Primary View – Multi-Line Graph**

**Axes**

* X Axis: Survey Runs  
* Y Axis: Driver Score (0–10)

**Behaviour**

* Each driver represented by unique colour.  
* Legend allows driver visibility toggling.

![][image16]

**Alternate View – Grouped Bar Chart**

**Axes**

* X Axis: Drivers  
* Grouped Bars: Survey Runs  
* Y Axis: Score

![][image17]

#### 

**Benchmark Overlay (Applicable to Grouped Bar Chart)**

An optional benchmark overlay can be enabled via a toggle.

Benchmark types:

* Organization Benchmark (company baseline)  
* Custom Benchmark (user-selected benchmark survey)  
* External Benchmark (if configured for the tenant)

Visual:

* Thin horizontal benchmark line per driver.  
* Tooltip shows:  
  * Driver Score (selected survey)  
  * Benchmark Score  
  * Variance (+/-)

**Section Download (Image)**

* Driver section includes: **Download as Image**  
* Export includes:  
  * Current view state (multi-line or grouped bar)  
  * Legend  
  * Benchmark overlay (if enabled)  
  * Compare context \+ timestamp

![][image18]

#### 

#### **Question-wise Overtime Analysis (Common Questions)**

Purpose: For selected surveys, allow overtime comparison of **common rating questions**.

Common question identification:

* If surveys are clones, match by internal question ID.  
* If not available, fallback to normalized question text match.  
* Only questions common across all selected surveys are shown.

UI:

* Dropdown: Select Question (searchable)

Chart:

* X axis: Survey Runs  
* Y axis: Score (Fixed 0–10)

Tooltip:

* Survey Name  
* Question Text  
* Score  
* Response Count  
* Survey Month and Year

Empty State:

* If no common questions exist, display:  
  * “No common questions found across selected surveys.”

**Section Download (Image)**

* Question-wise section includes: **Download as Image**

**![][image19]**

### **Time Trend Analysis**

User can select Monthly, Quarterly or annual.

#### **Engagement Survey Comparison**

Chart Type: Line Graph

X Axis: Selected Time Period Y Axis: Engagement Score (0–10)

Tooltip:

* Survey Name  
* Month & Year  
* Score  
* Response Count  
* Participation Rat

![][image20]

Section includes: **Download as Image**

#### **eNPS Survey Comparison**

Chart Type: Line Graph

X Axis:Selected Time Period Y Axis: eNPS Score

![][image21]

 Section includes: **Download as Image**

#### **Driver Survey Comparison**

Multi-line driver trend showing aggregated driver scores over time.

Includes driver selection and legend toggles.

Displays only the drivers that are common for all selected surveys.

![][image22]

Section includes: **Download as Image**

### **Focus Points**

#### **Purpose**

Surface **actionable focus areas** by highlighting where engagement, eNPS, and driver scores are **consistently improving or deteriorating over time**, across the **same dimensions and anonymity rules** used in the Overtime Analysis.

**Dimension Handling (Key Change)**

* The **Dimensions shown in Focus Points must strictly stem from the attributes selected** during the Overtime Analysis configuration.  
  * Includes both **default and custom attributes**.  
* Dimensions must **respect the configured anonymity thresholds**.  
  * Any dimension value that does not meet anonymity criteria is excluded or masked.  
* No implicit or hardcoded dimensions (e.g., Team, Location) should appear unless explicitly selected earlier.

**Data Logic (Overtime-Aware)**

* Change calculations should be based on:  
  * **Last 2 available data points.**  
* Scores should reflect:  
  * Absolute score  
  * % change  
  * `Direction (↑ / ↓)`

**Table Structure (Refined)**

**Columns:**

* **Driver**  
* **Dimension** (attribute \+ value)  
* **Outperforming**  
   Highest positive change over time  
* **Underperforming**  
   Largest negative change over time  
* Allow sorting by magnitude of change.

![][image23]

#### **Survey-wise Drill Down from Focus Points**

Enable users to understand *why* a driver appears as an outperforming or underperforming Focus Point by breaking down **survey-wise driver score changes** for the selected **dimension value**.

**Trigger & Entry Point**

* Clicking on any **Outperforming** or **Underperforming** value in the **Focus Points table** should open a **Drill Down modal**.

**Drill Down Context (Read-only)**

The modal should clearly display:

* **Driver:** Selected driver (e.g., Work Environment)  
* **Dimension:** `Selected attribute and value (e.g., Location → Pune)`  
* **Change Basis:** The surveys will be listed sorting most recent on top.  
  * Survey Name  
  * Score  
  * Change from previous survey

![][image24]

**Download as Image**

* Focus Points section includes **Download as Image**  
* PNG includes: table header \+ visible rows \+ compare context \+ timestamp

## **FR4 Download Report (Overtime Analysis)**

As a Super-Admin or Sub-Admin, I want to export the overtime analysis data into an editable format (XLS/CSV).

### **Revamped Behavior**

* Clicking **Download Report** should open a **context-aware export modal**.  
* The export must **inherit the current Overtime Analysis configuration**, including:  
  * Selected surveys  
  * Selected dimensions (default \+ custom)  
  * Applied anonymity thresholds  
  * Selected timeframe (Monthly / Quarterly / Yearly / By Survey)

### **Enhanced Modal Fields**

* **Metric**  
  Show options: Engagement, eNPS, Driver Scores  
* **Time Granularity**  
   Monthly / Quarterly / Yearly / By Survey (preselected from UI).  
* **Dimensions**  
   Read-only preview of included dimensions (derived from analysis setup).

### **Export Rules & Guardrails**

* Export must **respect anonymity rules**; masked or excluded data should not appear in the file.  
* The exported file should be clearly labeled:  
  * Metric  
  * Timeframe  
  * Included surveys  
  * Applied anonymity thresholds

**![][image25]**

**Case 1: Metric \= eNPS, Timeframe \= Monthly**

**Report:** Monthly eNPS Scores (Across Selected Surveys)  
**Surveys Included:** Pulse Q1, Pulse Q2, Annual 2024  
**Timeframe:** Monthly

| Month (YYYY-MM) | Survey Name | Survey End Month | Responses | Participants | eNPS Score | Promoters (\#) | Promoters (%) | Neutrals (\#) | Neutrals (%) | Detractors (\#) | Detractors (%) |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| 2025-10 | Culture Survey Q4 2025 (Anchor) | Oct 2025 | 420 | 510 | 32 | 220 | 52.40% | 120 | 28.60% | 80 | 19.00% |
| 2025-07 | Culture Survey Q3 2025 | Jul 2025 | 390 | 500 | 25 | 185 | 47.40% | 125 | 32.10% | 80 | 20.50% |
| 2025-04 | Culture Survey Q2 2025 | Apr 2025 | 360 | 480 | 18 | 160 | 44.40% | 120 | 33.30% | 80 | 22.20% |
| 2024-10 | Culture Survey Q4 2024 | Oct 2024 | 340 | 460 | 14 | 145 | 42.60% | 120 | 35.30% | 75 | 22.10% |

**Case 2: Metric \= Engagement, Timeframe \= Monthly**

**Report**: Monthly Engagement Scores (Attribute-wise)  
**Surveys** Included: Pulse Q1, Pulse Q2, Annual 2024  
**Timeframe**: Monthly  
**Selected Attributes**: Team, Region, Manager, Business Unit, Base Location

| Month | Survey Name | Team | Region | Manager | Business Unit | Base Location | Engagement Score |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| May 2024 | Pulse Q1 | Team Alpha | EMEA | Manager A | Sales | London | 7.8 |
| May 2024 | Pulse Q1 | Team Beta | APAC | Manager C | Support | Bangalore | 5.9 |
| Jun 2024 | Pulse Q1 | Team Alpha | EMEA | Manager A | Sales | London | 8.1 |
| Jun 2024 | Pulse Q1 | Team Beta | APAC | Manager C | Support | Bangalore | 5.6 |
| Jul 2024 | Pulse Q2 | Team Alpha | EMEA | Manager A | Sales | London | 7.5 |
| Aug 2024 | Pulse Q2 | Team Alpha | EMEA | Manager A | Sales | London | 8.4 |
| Sep 2024 | Annual 2024 | Team Alpha | EMEA | Manager A | Sales | London | 7.9 |

**Case 3: Metric \= Drivers, Timeframe \= Monthly**

**Report**: Monthly Drivers Scores (Attribute-wise)  
**Surveys** Included: Pulse Q1, Pulse Q2, Annual 2024  
**Timeframe**: Monthly

| Month (YYYY-MM) | Survey Name | Driver | Dimension | Dimension Value | Responses | Avg Driver Score (0–10) | Historical Avg (0–10) | Δ Score | Δ % |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| 2025-10 | Culture Survey Q4 2025 (Anchor) | Work Environment | Team | Operations | 88 | 7.6 | 6.8 | 0.8 | 0.118 |
| 2025-10 | Culture Survey Q4 2025 (Anchor) | Work Environment | Team | Quality Assurance | 74 | 6.2 | 6.9 | \-0.7 | \-10.10% |
| 2025-10 | Culture Survey Q4 2025 (Anchor) | Work Environment | Region | South | 95 | 7.1 | 6.6 | 0.5 | 0.076 |
| 2025-10 | Culture Survey Q4 2025 (Anchor) | Work Environment | Region | North | 80 | 6.4 | 6.8 | \-0.4 | \-5.90% |
| 2025-10 | Culture Survey Q4 2025 (Anchor) | Rewards | Manager | Ajay Singh | 52 | 7.4 | 6.5 | 0.9 | 0.138 |
| 2025-10 | Culture Survey Q4 2025 (Anchor) | Rewards | Manager | Rohit Gupta | 49 | 5.8 | 6.4 | \-0.6 | \-9.40% |
| 2025-10 | Culture Survey Q4 2025 (Anchor) | Growth & Development | Business Unit | Product | 70 | 7.8 | 6.7 | 1.1 | 0.164 |
| 2025-10 | Culture Survey Q4 2025 (Anchor) | Growth & Development | Base Location | Bengaluru | 85 | 7.2 | 6.8 | 0.4 | 0.059 |
| 2025-07 | Culture Survey Q3 2025 | Work Environment | Team | Operations | 82 | 7.1 | 6.4 | 0.7 | 0.109 |
